title: eriophyllum oppositifolium
intro: Eriophyllum oppositifolium, also known as Oregon sunshine, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is valued for its ability to reduce fever, soothe respiratory issues, and support the immune system, offering potential health benefits such as relief from colds, flu, and other infections. Traditionally, Oregon sunshine has been used to make herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be prepared by steeping the dried leaves and flowers in hot water or by combining them with other herbs in a tea blend.
